title: ChArUco Utilities
description: A complete ChArUco-based camera calibration toolkit for Unreal Engine. Generate boards, detect markers from live media feeds, solve intrinsics and stereo rigs, and export directly to UE native Lens Files.

sections.title: Overview
body: ChArUco Utilities is an Unreal Engine 5.3+ plugin designed to streamline camera calibration and tracking workflows. It provides a comprehensive toolset for generating ChArUco boards, detecting markers from live media feeds, solving camera intrinsics, and performing stereo camera calibration.

The plugin bridges the gap between OpenCV's powerful calibration algorithms and Unreal Engine's native CameraCalibration stack - suitable for Virtual Production (ICVFX), Augmented Reality, and computer vision research.

sections.title: Core Features
body: Integrated ChArUco Generation - Create and preview custom calibration boards directly in the editor with Spawn Board Actor for world-space alignment.
Live Detection - Real-time board detection from RenderTargets or MediaTextures (Live Link, Blackmagic, AJA feeds).
Sophisticated Solver - Calibrate camera intrinsics (Focal Length, Principal Point) and distortion coefficients k1-k6, p1-p2.
Stereo Calibration - Calculate the precise spatial transform between two cameras for multi-view rigs.
Lens File Integration - Export solved data directly into Unreal's native .uasset Lens Files.
Debug Visualization - Overlay 3D axes and board outlines onto your media feed for instant validation.

sections.title: Prerequisites
sections.body: The plugin depends on Unreal's internal camera calibration modules. Ensure CameraCalibration, CameraCalibrationCore, and OpenCV (included in UE5) are enabled via Edit → Plugins before installing.

sections.title: Installation
steps: Place the ChArUcoUtilities folder into your project's Plugins/ directory.
Windows: Ensure the engine's OpenCV module is functional.
Mac: The plugin uses vendored static libraries in ThirdParty/OpenCV/Mac.
Restart the editor and rebuild the project when prompted.

sections.title: Accessing the Tool
body: Once installed, open the main interface via the Level Editor Toolbar (click the ChArUco icon) or from Window → ChArUco Utility.

sections.title: Board Configuration
steps: Navigate to the Board Setup section in the widget.
Define your grid - e.g. Cols: 5, Rows: 7 - and set Square/Marker Size in MM.
Physical dimensions must match your printed board exactly for accurate pose scale.
Click Generate Preview to verify your board matches the plugin settings.

sections.title: Capturing Views
steps: In the Detection section, select your Source Media Texture or Render Target.
Position the physical board in front of the camera.
When the board is detected (visible in the Debug View), click Capture View.
Repeat from varied angles covering all corners of the frame - aim for 20+ captures.

sections.title: Solving and Applying
steps: Under Calibration, select your Distortion Model (Default is usually best).
Click Solve Calibration and check the RMS Error - ideally < 0.5 px.
Assign a Target Camera Actor and click Apply Pose to apply the calibration.

sections.caption: Function buttons at the bottom of the widget UI

sections.title: Stereo Calibration
body: For stereo rigs (e.g. a secondary tracking camera or a 3D pair): calibrate Camera 1 and Camera 2 individually first to establish their intrinsics, then assign both feeds in the Stereo Calibration section to solve the spatial transform between them.

sections.body: Target RMS < 0.5 px for a reliable calibration. Use at least 20 varied board poses covering all corners and angles. Avoid motion blur and keep the board in sharp focus. Use Load Preset / Save Preset to store your board configuration for reuse across sessions.

sections.title: Blueprint API
body: The UChArUcoBlueprintFunctionLib provides functions for custom calibration logic at runtime - enabling integration into automated pipelines, custom UI widgets, and production tools built on top of the plugin.
